query: Review all the fracture finding models in defs/ and flag anything whose change-from-prior values look wrong.
Audit defs/*_hemorrhage.fm.json for naming issues, suspect synonyms, and unnecessary direction-of-change pairs.
Walk through every finding model tagged 'brain' and tell me what needs improvement.
Please look at defs/subdural_hematoma.fm.json, defs/epidural_hematoma.fm.json, and defs/subarachnoid_hemorrhage.fm.json and clean them up.
I want to do a quality pass over the knee MRI finding models — can you walk through them with me via the TUI?
Audit the existing cardiac finding models for naming conventions and missing synonyms.
Check all files matching defs/*_device.fm.json for inappropriate direction-of-change values. Devices shouldn't 'worsen' or get 'larger'.
Run a cleanup pass over defs/ — specifically the pulmonary nodule models. Look for component attributes that should be extracted into their own models.
Review defs/pneumocephalus.fm.json and defs/tension_pneumocephalus.fm.json — I want to make sure they aren't duplicating each other improperly.
Go through all existing brain volumetry models in defs/ and propose improvements to their descriptions and synonyms.
Do we already have a finding model for cerebral microbleeds? If not, please add one.
Process this CSV of 20 new head CT findings and create models for anything not already in the library.
Add 'SDH' as a synonym to the subdural hematoma model.
I want to create a new finding model for pneumocephalus.
Here's a directory of markdown stubs — please batch-create finding models for all of them.
Run scripts/validator.py and tell me if any models fail validation.
Write a Python tool to count how many finding models exist per tag.
Explain how the OIFM ID format works.
Check whether the pleural effusion model exists in the library.
Help me write a commit message for my latest changes to defs/.
